data.title: Taboola’s DeeperDive AI Aims To Turn Publisher Queries Into High‑Intent Ads
data.description:
• Taboola.com (NasdaqGS:TBLA) has launched DeeperDive, a generative AI powered answer engine.
  • The tool is integrated with HuffPost UK, giving readers conversational, contextual responses directly on the publisher site.
  • The launch is designed to create new high intent ad inventory inside publisher environments.

For readers tracking NasdaqGS:TBLA, this launch comes with the stock at $3.75 and a mixed return profile. Shares are up 11.9% over the past week and 21.0% over the past month, while longer periods show a 34.9% gain over 1 year and a 58.2% gain over 3 years, set against a 15.3% decline year to date and a 63.3% decline over 5 years.

The DeeperDive rollout reflects how Taboola is trying to keep users engaged on publisher sites while creating fresh ad opportunities tied to explicit user intent. For investors, a central consideration is whether broader adoption of this kind of generative AI experience across Taboola's publisher base can result in more resilient, higher quality revenue over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Taboola.com by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Taboola.com.

3 things going right for Taboola.com that this headline doesn't cover.

For Taboola.com, DeeperDive sits right in the middle of where investors are already focused: using AI-powered tools to keep users on the open web and capture more advertiser demand that might otherwise go to search engines or social platforms like Alphabet, Meta Platforms or Snap. Because DeeperDive runs on publisher sites and uses their own content, it can help partners such as HuffPost UK keep reader journeys on site instead of sending them out to external search results. That aligns with Taboola’s role as an open web monetization partner and leans on its existing scale across 9,000 publishers and 600 million daily active users described in the announcement.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Taboola.com Narrative

  • The DeeperDive launch supports the narrative that new platforms can expand Taboola’s addressable market beyond traditional native ads, by creating search like, high intent ad inventory directly on publisher pages.
  • Execution risk is a recurring theme in the narrative and this product adds another layer, because broad publisher adoption and advertiser uptake of these AI-powered answer experiences will be needed to justify the more optimistic growth assumptions.
  • The narrative focuses heavily on Realize and device partnerships, while DeeperDive introduces a separate AI answer engine that could influence how users find content and how budgets are allocated, which may not yet be fully reflected in those earlier assumptions.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Taboola.com to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ DeeperDive relies on large publishers choosing to integrate AI-powered answer experiences, so slower than expected adoption or shorter contract terms could limit the revenue opportunity and increase volatility in publisher relationships.
  • ⚠️ The product targets high intent, search like advertising budgets that also attract large competitors such as Alphabet and other ad tech platforms, which could pressure pricing and make it harder for Taboola to sustain attractive take rates.
  • 🎁 If DeeperDive helps publishers keep users on site longer and surface more relevant content, it could strengthen Taboola’s role as a core monetization partner and support the recent pattern of revenue growth reported over the last two years.
  • 🎁 The ability to insert contextually relevant ads directly into answer results may create an additional, high intent revenue stream that complements Taboola’s existing content recommendation units and broad publisher network.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, the key things to track are how many publishers adopt DeeperDive, how prominently they surface it within their sites and whether advertisers start to treat this inventory more like search, with higher cost per click or cost per action budgets. It is also worth watching whether Taboola reports any contribution metrics tied to AI-powered products in future updates, such as engagement, time on site or revenue mix. Any further rollouts with other major publishers or categories like travel, sports and finance would give a clearer picture of how scalable this model is alongside existing partnerships and the Realize platform.

data.title: Is Atmus Filtration Technologies (ATMU) Still Attractive After A 90% One-Year Share Price Jump
data.description:
• Investors may be wondering whether Atmus Filtration Technologies, with a last close of US$63.38, still offers value or whether most of the opportunity is already priced in.
  • The stock has posted returns of 0.3% over the last 7 days, 11.8% over the last 30 days, 20.9% year to date and 90.7% over the last year. These figures naturally raise questions about what is driving sentiment and how much is already reflected in the price.

The next sections will break down common valuation methods before finishing with a more holistic way to think about what the stock might be worth.

Atmus Filtration Technologies scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Atmus Filtration Technologies Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes forecasts of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today using a required rate of return. The idea is simple: what matters for you as a shareholder is the cash the business can return over time, expressed in today’s dollars.

For Atmus Filtration Technologies, Simply Wall St uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model based on projected Free Cash Flow (FCF) in $. The latest twelve months FCF is about $151.0 million. Analyst estimates extend out to 2027, with FCF for that year projected at $251.4 million. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates ten year FCF projections, with discounted values provided for each year from 2026 through 2035.

Adding those discounted cash flows and a terminal value together gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $71.08 per share. Versus the recent share price of $63.38, this implies a 10.8% discount, suggesting the stock is currently priced below this DCF estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Atmus Filtration Technologies is undervalued by 10.8%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 59 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Atmus Filtration Technologies.

Approach 2: Atmus Filtration Technologies Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It lets you compare different businesses on a like for like basis and it is widely used across the Machinery industry.

What counts as a “fair” P/E ratio often reflects the earnings growth investors expect and how risky they think those earnings are. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while lower growth expectations or higher risk tend to line up with a lower multiple.

Atmus Filtration Technologies currently trades on a P/E of 24.96x. That sits below the Machinery industry average of 27.56x, but above the peer group average of 20.98x. Simply Wall St also calculates a Fair Ratio of 21.10x, which is the P/E it would expect for Atmus Filtration Technologies given factors such as its earnings growth profile, margins, size, industry and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio can be more useful than a simple peer or industry comparison because it attempts to tailor the “right” multiple to the company’s own fundamentals rather than using a broad average.

With a current P/E of 24.96x versus a Fair Ratio of 21.10x, the shares screen as trading above this fair value estimate.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives are an easy tool used by millions of investors that connects a company’s qualitative story to a quantified forecast and a fair value range, updating automatically as fresh information such as news, guidance or earnings arrives.

data.title: Here's Why We Think Lifco (STO:LIFCO B) Might Deserve Your Attention Today
data.description:
It's common for many investors, especially those who are inexperienced, to buy shares in companies with a good story even if these companies are loss-making. Sometimes these stories can cloud the minds of investors, leading them to invest with their emotions rather than on the merit of good company fundamentals. Loss making companies can act like a sponge for capital - so investors should be cautious that they're not throwing good money after bad.

So if this idea of high risk and high reward doesn't suit, you might be more interested in profitable, growing companies, like Lifco (STO:LIFCO B). Now this is not to say that the company presents the best investment opportunity around, but profitability is a key component to success in business.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Lifco's Earnings Per Share Are Growing

If you believe that markets are even vaguely efficient, then over the long term you'd expect a company's share price to follow its earnings per share (EPS) outcomes. That means EPS growth is considered a real positive by most successful long-term investors. Over the last three years, Lifco has grown EPS by 9.3% per year. That's a good rate of growth, if it can be sustained.

Careful consideration of revenue growth and earnings before interest and taxation (EBIT) margins can help inform a view on the sustainability of the recent profit growth. Lifco maintained stable EBIT margins over the last year, all while growing revenue 8.1% to kr28b. That's progress.

You can take a look at the company's revenue and earnings growth trend, in the chart below. To see the actual numbers, click on the chart.

Fortunately, we've got access to analyst forecasts of Lifco's future profits. You can do your own forecasts without looking, or you can take a peek at what the professionals are predicting.

Are Lifco Insiders Aligned With All Shareholders?

Investors are always searching for a vote of confidence in the companies they hold and insider buying is one of the key indicators for optimism on the market. This view is based on the possibility that stock purchases signal bullishness on behalf of the buyer. Of course, we can never be sure what insiders are thinking, we can only judge their actions.

The real kicker here is that Lifco insiders spent a staggering kr44m on acquiring shares in just one year, without single share being sold in the meantime. The shareholders within the general public should find themselves expectant and certainly hopeful, that this large outlay signals prescient optimism for the business. We also note that it was the CEO, President & Director, Per Waldemarson, who made the biggest single acquisition, paying kr15m for shares at about kr301 each.

On top of the insider buying, we can also see that Lifco insiders own a large chunk of the company. Indeed, with a collective holding of 51%, company insiders are in control and have plenty of capital behind the venture. Intuition will tell you this is a good sign because it suggests they will be incentivised to build value for shareholders over the long term. data.title: Chemung Financial (CHMG) Net Margin Compression Reinforces Bearish Earnings Narratives
data.description:
Chemung Financial (CHMG) opened the new reporting year with Q1 2026 results that sit against a recent run of quarterly revenue between US$23.7 million and US$29.4 million and basic EPS that has ranged from a profit of about US$1.63 to a loss of US$1.34. Over the past few quarters, the company has seen total revenue move from US$23.7 million in Q3 2024 to US$25.3 million in Q4 2024 and then to US$29.4 million in Q4 2025, while basic EPS shifted from US$1.20 to US$1.29 and then to US$1.63 over the same periods, setting the backdrop for how investors judge the latest quarter. With a trailing net profit margin of 16.7% and earnings growth concerns already in focus, the fresh numbers land in a context where compression in profitability is a key part of the story.

See our full analysis for Chemung Financial.

With the headline figures in place, the next step is to weigh them against the most common narratives around Chemung Financial to see which storylines hold up and which ones the latest earnings call into question.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Loan book climbs toward US$2.3b

  • Total loans moved from US$2,028.9 million in Q3 2024 to US$2,269.6 million by Q4 2025, with non performing loans in that period ranging between US$7.8 million and US$10.5 million.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is that higher loan balances sit alongside lower non performing loans over the last twelve months. Loans were at US$2,269.6 million and non performing loans at US$7.9 million in Q4 2025, which investors can compare with earlier quarters where loans were smaller and non performing balances reached US$10.5 million.

Margins and costs find a middle ground

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, net interest margin was 3.26% and the cost to income ratio was 63%. This compares with earlier trailing data points that show net interest margin at 2.76% and cost to income at 68.89% in Q4 2024.
  • Bears focus on the 9.5% annual decline in trailing earnings and the net profit margin moving from 24.3% to 16.7%. However, the improvement in net interest margin from 2.76% to 3.26% and the lower cost to income ratio at 63% suggest operating spread and efficiency were not moving in the same direction as headline profitability, which gives you a more mixed picture than a simple earnings decline story.
    • The 16.7% net profit margin over the last year still leaves room above many low margin businesses, even if it is below the prior 24.3% level that bears highlight.
    • Cost to income moving from 68.89% to 63% on a trailing basis shows expenses consumed a smaller share of income, which partially offsets the bearish point about weaker bottom line trends.

Rich P/E meets discounted DCF fair value

  • The shares trade at US$62.82 with a P/E of 20x, compared with a DCF fair value of US$95.39 and a sector context where the US Banks industry sits at 11.9x P/E and peers at 15.3x.
  • What creates tension for both bullish and bearish views is that the DCF fair value suggests about a 34.1% gap between price and that estimate, while the 20x P/E is above industry and peer levels. Investors weighing the 2.16% dividend yield and the longer term 9.5% annual earnings decline have to decide whether the discounted DCF fair value or the richer earnings multiple is more important for their own valuation work.
    • Bears point to the higher 20x P/E versus 11.9x for the broader US Banks industry as a sign the stock is not cheap on simple multiples, even with earnings under pressure.
    • Bulls counter that a 34.1% discount to the DCF fair value and ongoing dividends at a 2.16% yield provide some support, even if earnings and margins have come down from earlier levels.

data.title: Impressive Earnings May Not Tell The Whole Story For BHG Group (STO:BHG)

The Impact Of Unusual Items On Profit

For anyone who wants to understand BHG Group's profit beyond the statutory numbers, it's important to note that during the last twelve months statutory profit gained from kr77m worth of unusual items. We can't deny that higher profits generally leave us optimistic, but we'd prefer it if the profit were to be sustainable. We ran the numbers on most publicly listed companies worldwide, and it's very common for unusual items to be once-off in nature. And, after all, that's exactly what the accounting terminology implies. Assuming those unusual items don't show up again in the current year, we'd thus expect profit to be weaker next year (in the absence of business growth, that is).

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

Our Take On BHG Group's Profit Performance

Arguably, BHG Group's statutory earnings have been distorted by unusual items boosting profit. Therefore, it seems possible to us that BHG Group's true underlying earnings power is actually less than its statutory profit. On the bright side, the company showed enough improvement to book a profit this year, after losing money last year. Of course, we've only just scratched the surface when it comes to analysing its earnings; one could also consider margins, forecast growth, and return on investment, among other factors.
